title: Align checkpoint events with the minimal canonical core event set
goal: Align the runtime event model with the minimal canonical checkpoint set defined in the plan so deterministic continuation depends on begin, verify, commit, and end checkpoints only.
scope: Identify the current checkpoint and progress event shapes emitted by runtime code
Define the minimal canonical event set required for deterministic continuation
Remove or demote non-essential progress-style events from the protocol core
Ensure no-change verified completion can proceed without commit checkpoint events
Document which events remain canonical and which are optional observability events
out_of_scope: Changing materialized state schema beyond what is required to align event semantics
Implementing regression tests beyond minimal validation needed to support this ticket
inputs: runtime event append and replay code paths
expected_outputs: A runtime event model aligned to the minimal canonical core checkpoint set
A clear distinction between canonical checkpoint events and optional observability events
Updated code comments or documentation where the event contract is defined
A list of any legacy or optional events that remain supported but are no longer protocol-defining
acceptance_criteria: The canonical core checkpoint set is limited to tx.begin, tx.verify.start, tx.verify.pass, tx.verify.fail, tx.commit.start, tx.commit.done, tx.commit.fail, tx.end.done, and tx.end.blocked
Deterministic continuation no longer depends on generic progress-style events
Optional observability events, if retained, are explicitly non-canonical for resumable continuation
A verified transaction with no repository changes can proceed directly to explicit terminal completion without tx.commit.start or tx.commit.done
The resulting event model is consistent with the minimal canonical continuation contract and the next_action-driven resume model
notes: This ticket should be implemented only after the identity boundary and minimal continuation contract are already explicit in code.
If legacy event shapes remain readable for compatibility, they must not be allowed to redefine the new canonical event contract.
This ticket is about protocol-defining checkpoint events, not about removing all auxiliary logging or observability events.
